label: 2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 17

extraction.one_line_summary: ROGLIČ attacked on the Angliru's brutal ramps, took the stage with VINGEGAARD, while KUSS held red by 8 seconds after appearing to ask teammates to slow down.
tactical_patterns: KUSS radioed asking for slower pace when VINGEGAARD and ROGLIČ attacked, revealing internal team tension on steepest gradients.
VINGEGAARD and ROGLIČ collected 16 combined bonus seconds without allowing KUSS to contest sprint, coordinating GC assault despite teammate in red.
Bahrain Victorious exhausted CARUSO and POELS pacing for LANDA before steepest ramps, leaving LANDA isolated when attacks came.
SOLER bridged to breakaway mid-stage on category one climb despite team strategy being unclear, aggressive move before final climb.

rider_signals.KUSS Sepp: negative: Requesting slower pace while teammates attacked suggests he couldn't follow their acceleration on 23% ramps, vulnerability exposed.
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: Attacking and winning on Angliru's steepest sections despite being 1:08 down shows superior climbing form returning late-race.
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: positive: Coordinating attack with ROGLIČ while ignoring race leader teammate indicates GC ambitions prioritized over team hierarchy.
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: negative: Isolated after teammates emptied themselves pacing too early, unable to follow accelerations on steepest 23% pitches.
rider_signals.AYUSO Juan: negative: Lost significant time to drop from 4th to losing podium spot, Angliru gradients exposed his limits.
rider_signals.SOLER Marc: neutral: Mid-race bridge to breakaway on category one suggests either confused team tactics or individual opportunism before final climb.
rider_signals.UIJTDEBROEKS Cian: positive: Strong ride setting tempo for BORA before final climb despite being young domestique on hardest stage.
